product_overview: A responsive single-page app graph builder for visualizing application service dependencies with ReactFlow. Users can choose a mock app, inspect and edit a selected service node, and toggle a simulated API error mode for loading-state testing.
core_goals: Select a mock application from a list
View the selected application's service dependency graph
Inspect and edit a service node's configuration and runtime details
Pan, zoom, and fit the graph to the viewport
Use a mobile-friendly slide-over panel on smaller screens
Toggle mock API error simulation to observe loading and error states

features.name: App Selection
features.description: Browse the available mock applications and switch the graph view to the selected app.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Wait for the applications list to load -> Select an application from the list -> See the selected application highlighted -> See the graph refresh for the chosen application
Navigate to / -> Wait for the applications list to load -> Select a different application from the list -> See the selection update in the list -> See the graph update for the new application
Navigate to / -> Trigger app loading in an error state -> Retry loading the applications -> See the applications list load successfully

features.name: Graph Canvas
features.description: View and interact with the service dependency graph for the selected application.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> View the service graph with nodes and edges -> Pan and zoom around the canvas -> Fit the graph to the viewport
Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Click a service node -> See the node become selected -> Click the canvas background -> See the selection clear
Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Trigger graph loading in an error state -> Retry loading the graph -> See the graph render successfully

features.name: Node Inspector
features.description: Inspect a selected service node and edit its basic configuration and runtime-related values.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Click a service node -> View the inspector for that node -> Switch between Config and Runtime tabs -> See the active tab content change
Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Click a service node -> Change the node name -> Change the node description -> See the inspector reflect the edited values

Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Click a service node -> Adjust the load percentage with the slider -> Enter a matching numeric value -> See the two controls stay in sync -> Observe the updated node details in the inspector
Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Click a service node -> View the node status pill -> View the runtime metrics

features.name: Simulate API Error
features.description: Toggle a mock error mode to observe how the app behaves when application and graph data requests are failing in the mocked environment.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enable simulated error mode -> See the applications list enter an error state -> Disable simulated error mode -> See normal loading behavior return
Navigate to / -> Select an application from the list -> Enable simulated error mode -> See the graph area enter an error state -> Disable simulated error mode -> See the graph load normally again

features.name: Mobile Panel
features.description: Use the responsive slide-over panel to access the applications list and node inspector on smaller screens.
user_flows: Open the app on a small screen -> Open the slide-over panel -> View the applications list and inspector in the drawer -> Select an application -> Select a node -> See the inspector open in the panel
Open the app on a small screen -> Select a node on the canvas -> See the mobile panel open automatically -> Review the selected node details

routes.description: Single-page App Graph Builder with service dependency visualization

features.name: App Selection
features.description: Load and switch between mock applications from the right panel apps list
user_interactions: View loading skeletons while apps load
Click an app in the Apps list to select it
See the selected app highlighted in the list
Click Retry on error state to refetch apps

features.name: Graph Canvas
features.description: Visualize service dependency graphs with ReactFlow nodes and edges
user_interactions: View graph nodes and edges after selecting an app
Click a node to select it and open the inspector
Click canvas background to deselect node
Pan and zoom the graph using controls
Use Fit button in top bar to fit graph to view
Click Retry on graph load error

features.name: Node Inspector
features.description: Inspect and edit selected service node configuration and runtime info
user_interactions: Select a node on canvas to view inspector
Switch between Config and Runtime tabs
Edit node name and description fields
Adjust load percentage via slider or numeric input
View status pill and runtime metrics

features.name: Simulate API Error
features.description: Toggle mock API error simulation from the top bar
user_interactions: Click Simulate Error button to enable error mode
See error states in apps list and graph canvas
Click Errors On button to disable error simulation

features.name: Mobile Panel
features.description: Responsive slide-over panel for apps and inspector on smaller screens
user_interactions: Click panel toggle button on mobile viewport
Open slide-over drawer with apps list and inspector
Select node on mobile to auto-open panel

known_limitations.issue: Inspector edits are lost when switching apps or refetching the graph
known_limitations.impact: Local node data changes are not persisted to the mock API

known_limitations.issue: Left rail navigation icons are non-functional placeholders
known_limitations.impact: Navigation icons do not navigate anywhere

known_limitations.issue: Share button in top bar is non-functional
known_limitations.impact: Share button has no action attached

known_limitations.issue: Node deletion via keyboard is disabled
known_limitations.impact: deleteKeyCode is set to null, nodes cannot be deleted
